structures.name: Freytag's Pyramid
structures.description: A classic five- (or seven-)part arc from exposition through rising action, climax, and resolution. It turns 19th-century dramatic analysis into a practical map for pacing tension and payoff in fiction, theatre, and film.
structures.author: After Gustav Freytag’s model of dramatic structure, widely used in writing craft.

phases.title: Exposition
phases.description: Establish setting, characters, and the ordinary world before tension takes hold.

phases.title: Inciting Incident
phases.description: An event disrupts equilibrium and launches the central conflict.

phases.title: Rising Action
phases.description: Complications escalate as obstacles, choices, and stakes intensify.

phases.title: Climax
phases.description: The story’s turning point of highest tension and decisive confrontation.

phases.title: Falling Action
phases.description: Consequences unfold and momentum moves toward a settled outcome.

phases.title: Resolution
phases.description: The main conflict resolves and a new stable situation emerges.

phases.title: Denouement
phases.description: Final emotional closure and the sense of the new normal after the storm.
